title: Posterior Urethral Valves: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Posterior Urethral Valves
Identify key diagnostic features of Posterior Urethral Valves
Apply clinical reasoning to Posterior Urethral Valves management
Recognize common pitfalls in Posterior Urethral Valves

sections.title: Overview of Posterior Urethral Valves
sections.content:
**Posterior Urethral Valves**



**Key Points:**
• Most common cause of lower urinary tract obstruction in male neonates
• Prenatal: bilateral hydronephrosis, distended bladder (keyhole sign), oligohydramnios
• VCUG is diagnostic: dilated posterior urethra, trabeculated bladder

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Most common cause of lower urinary tract obstruction in male neonates
• Prenatal: bilateral hydronephrosis, distended bladder (keyhole sign), oligohydramnios

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• VCUG is diagnostic: dilated posterior urethra, trabeculated bladder
• Treatment: catheter drainage → cystoscopic valve ablation
• Long-term: monitor renal function, risk of CKD and valve bladder syndrome

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Posterior Urethral Valves:**

• VCUG is diagnostic: dilated posterior urethra, trabeculated bladder
• Treatment: catheter drainage → cystoscopic valve ablation
• Long-term: monitor renal function, risk of CKD and valve bladder syndrome

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

options.explanation: Most common cause of lower urinary tract obstruction in male neonates

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Most common cause of lower urinary tract obstruction in male neonates.
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Most common cause of lower urinary tract obstruction in male neonates

coachDx.misconception: While typically self-resolving without treatment may seem plausible, it does not match the clinical scenario described.
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Most common cause of lower urinary tract obstruction in male neonates from Typically self-resolving without treatment.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Most common cause of lower urinary tract obstruction in male neonates.
